reasoning: The Federal Reserve recently initiated an easing cycle, cutting the federal funds rate by 25 basis points on September 17, 2025, bringing the target range to 4.00%-4.25% and the overnight reverse repo (ON RRP) award rate to 4.00%. The RRP rate strictly follows the bottom of the federal funds target range during normal policy regimes (any technical adjustment would likely be downward, to push balances out of the facility, rather than upward). The FOMC's median dot plot and market pricing strongly predict further rate cuts over the remainder of 2025, specifically forecasting additional 25-50 basis points in cuts by the end of the year. With the next meetings on October 28-29 and December 10, the policy rate is widely expected to either hold steady or decrease further by our resolution dates (Nov 2, Nov 25, Jan 24). For the RRP award rate to increase above its October 26 baseline of 4.00%, the Fed would have to reverse course and unexpectedly hike rates—an extraordinarily unlikely event barring an immediate, catastrophic inflationary shock. Therefore, the chance of the rate increasing is near the 5% lower bound, with slight increases in uncertainty for the later resolution dates.
